summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of an epic New Year's marathon – Shakedown Street, Dark Star and the Other One/NFA/Goin’ Down the Road/Dew sequences soar.
review: The GD‑81‑12‑31 recording is a pristine SBD that reproduces every nuance of what many fans call one of the greatest NYE shows of the ’80s.  Highlights include a monster 16‑minute Shakedown Street, the first post‑’70 Dark Star (and its fiery Bertha/Good Lovin' pairing), and an unstoppable Other One → NFA → Goin’ Down the Road → Dew stretch that drives the set flow.  The only blemishes are the much‑criticized Joan Baez acoustic segment and a brief “dying cat” harmonica glitch on Iko, but they do little to mar the overall brilliance.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive reviews (≈85% 4‑5 stars) praise both performance and soundboard quality; minor complaints are limited to the acoustic set and a single harmonica glitch.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, balanced mix with clear instrument separation; occasional minor issue noted on Iko (harmonica ‘dying‑cat’ sound) but otherwise flawless.

standout_songs: The Other One > Not Fade Away > Goin' Down the Road > Morning Dew
show_quality.setlist_flow: Four‑set marathon flows superbly: a powerhouse first set, an ultra‑jammy second set anchored by Shakedown, a space‑filled third set with Terrapin/Playin' and the Other One suite, and a historic Dark Star/Bertha encore that caps the night.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for crisp guitar tone, inspired solos (especially on Shakedown) and strong vocals throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for tight intro to The Other One and emotive lines on Morning Dew; occasional mention of his “smooth” space work.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned briefly as part of the GDTRFB jam (“you can really hear Bob in this version”).
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland highlighted for solid playing on GDTRFB and overall groove; Keith is praised in a nostalgic comment but not directly relevant to this show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart receive kudos for the dynamic drums‑>space transition leading into The Other One.
